creator: damjan.jov@gmail.com
text:
Repeating that test with a 64GiB loop mounted disk image:

# dd if=/dev/zero of=64G bs=1G count=64
# newfs_msdos ./64G
# mdconfig -a -f ./64G
# mount -t msdosfs /dev/md0 /mnt/test
# mkdir /mnt/test/ports
# cp -r /usr/ports/* /mnt/test/ports

After about 2.5 hours that took, only the following directories come with garbage filenames, that weren't present in /usr/ports:

ports/devel (6065 entries)
ports/games (1203 entries)
ports/graphics (1124 entries)
ports/security (1280 entries)
ports/sysutils (1368 entries)
ports/textproc (1804 entries)
ports/www (2569 entries)

The prior newfs_msdos command gave me this output:
./64G: 134184896 sectors in 2096639 FAT32 clusters (32768 bytes/cluster)

The size of an 8.3 filename directory entry is 32 bytes (longer filenames span multiple directory entries), and 32 * 1024 = the 32768 byte limit in 1 cluster, meaning a directory of > 1024 entries has to extend over multiple clusters. It is these and only these directories that got the corrupt entries.
